result.name: Edric Greymark

sections.setting: Lands of Legends: A mythic fantasy world shaped by ancient migrations, damaged Gates, and conflicting origin myths. Tone and style: Epic, mystical, grounded, ancient-but-lived-in, high fantasy with mythic ambiguity. Cultures feel old, layered, and internally consistent. Core world truths: The Alfirin are immortal exiles who entered this world through Gates after a failed negotiation with the Velim, damaging reality. The Velim are ancient custodians of Gates and thresholds, forced into guardianship after the Alfirin passage went wrong. History is fragmented; myths contradict; no single culture is fully correct. Cultural themes: ancient wounds still shaping the present, survival over destiny, balance versus permanence, quiet guardianship versus visible power, myth over absolute truth.
sections.appearance: expressive faces, weathered mortal features, lean athletic build, quick hands, sharp features, alert predatory confidence
sections.clothing: culturally varied practical garments shaped by climate and profession, fitted leathers, layered travel garments, elegant but practical clothing suited to stealth, deception, and sudden movement
sections.weapons_props: narrow rapier, coin purses and hidden pockets
sections.pose: balanced stance with weapon loosely ready
sections.environment: Wind-carved mountains where nomadic human clans and Highland Uruk share the land. Stone circles, ritual tattoos, and ancestral memory define a harsh but spiritual landscape.
sections.lighting: strong directional light with deep shadows
sections.layout: clean full-body presentation with readable equipment and minimal background clutter
sections.race_flavor: Core truths: Adaptable and short-lived.; Diverse cultures filling the spaces left by elder stagnation.. Themes: ambition, resilience, contradiction, cultural friction. Tone: grounded, passionate, volatile. Visual cues: scale human; skin palette varied; signature features expressive faces, weathered mortal features. Avoid: portraying humans as weak, portraying humans as secondary to elder races.
sections.style: painted british comic illustration, gritty graphic novel art, heavy ink outlines, thick painterly brush strokes, high contrast saturated colors, dramatic low-angle perspective, exaggerated anatomy, chaotic layered backgrounds, heavy shadows, glowing highlights, metal textures and worn surfaces, dark fantasy graphic novel illustration

result.text:
Dark fantasy character prompt.

Setting:
Lands of Legends: A mythic fantasy world shaped by ancient migrations, damaged Gates, and conflicting origin myths. Tone and style: Epic, mystical, grounded, ancient-but-lived-in, high fantasy with mythic ambiguity. Cultures feel old, layered, and internally consistent. Core world truths: The Alfirin are immortal exiles who entered this world through Gates after a failed negotiation with the Velim, damaging reality. The Velim are ancient custodians of Gates and thresholds, forced into guardianship after the Alfirin passage went wrong. History is fragmented; myths contradict; no single culture is fully correct. Cultural themes: ancient wounds still shaping the present, survival over destiny, balance versus permanence, quiet guardianship versus visible power, myth over absolute truth.

Character:
Edric Greymark, female human rogue.

Appearance:
expressive faces, weathered mortal features, lean athletic build, quick hands, sharp features, alert predatory confidence.

Clothing:
culturally varied practical garments shaped by climate and profession, fitted leathers, layered travel garments, elegant but practical clothing suited to stealth, deception, and sudden movement.

Weapons / Props:
narrow rapier, coin purses and hidden pockets.

Pose:
balanced stance with weapon loosely ready.

Environment:
Wind-carved mountains where nomadic human clans and Highland Uruk share the land. Stone circles, ritual tattoos, and ancestral memory define a harsh but spiritual landscape.

Lighting:
strong directional light with deep shadows.

Layout:
clean full-body presentation with readable equipment and minimal background clutter.

Race Flavor:
Core truths: Adaptable and short-lived.; Diverse cultures filling the spaces left by elder stagnation.. Themes: ambition, resilience, contradiction, cultural friction. Tone: grounded, passionate, volatile. Visual cues: scale human; skin palette varied; signature features expressive faces, weathered mortal features. Avoid: portraying humans as weak, portraying humans as secondary to elder races.

STYLE:
painted british comic illustration, gritty graphic novel art, heavy ink outlines, thick painterly brush strokes, high contrast saturated colors, dramatic low-angle perspective, exaggerated anatomy, chaotic layered backgrounds, heavy shadows, glowing highlights, metal textures and worn surfaces, dark fantasy graphic novel illustration.
